title: segfault when using md5 on unbound variable in conductor sparql
description:
We experienced a segfault when executing the following query from the conductor sparql and isql interface. It was handled correctly on the public sparql endpoint.
PREFIX dcat: <http://www.w3.org/ns/dcat#>
PREFIX dct: <http://purl.org/dc/terms/>
INSERT INTO <http://my-url/distributions/> {
?distribution a dcat:Distribution.
?distribution dct:title ?title.
}
WHERE {
?ds a dcat:Distribution .
?ds dct:title ?title .
BIND (IRI(CONCAT("http://my-url/distributions/",md5(?id))) AS ?distribution). }

Expected result (returned on public /sparql)
Virtuoso 37000 Error SP031: SPARQL compiler: Variable 'id' is used in the query result set but not assigned

Returned result
virtuoso-t[17175]: segfault at 7fcb10279000 ip 00007fcb9ae6ca6b sp 00007fcb278628d0 error 4 in libcrypto.so.1.0.1e[7fcb9adfe000+1b5000]

Virtuoso version: 6.1.8 stable
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE distributions (id INT PRIMARY KEY, title VARCHAR(255), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO distributions (id, title) VALUES (1, 'Distribution One');
INSERT INTO distributions (id, title) VALUES (2, NULL);
SELECT id, title, MD5(IFNULL(title, 'default')) AS title_hash FROM distributions WHERE id = 1;
INSERT INTO distributions (id, title) SELECT 3, CONCAT('Distribution ', id) FROM distributions WHERE id < 3;
key_bug_features: segfault using md5 on unbound variable in query
